# SKANF SUCCESS OUTPUT FIXTURE
# Contract: ForwarderA — execute(address target, bytes calldata data) → target.call(data)
# Contract bytecode selector: 0x1cff79cd
# Pipeline: analyze_hex.sh (Phase 1) + greed --find 0x76 (Phase 2, directed search)
#
# NOTE: "Success" in SKANF means greed found a concrete execution path to the target
# CALL instruction and generated concrete calldata to reach it.
# The --find <callPC> flag enables directed search toward the vulnerable CALL.
#
# Phase 1 stdout (analyze_hex.sh):
Running gigahorse.py
Yes
#
# Phase 2 stderr (greed symbolic execution with --find 0x76):
INFO | greed | Found State 7 at 0x76
INFO | greed | CALLDATA: 
#
# Interpretation of success fields:
#   callPC            → 0x76 (the CALL TAC statement ID)
#   calldata          → 1cff79cd00...00 (concrete hex calldata to reach the CALL)
#   function selector → 0x1cff79cd = execute(address,bytes)
#   target address    → symbolic (0x00...00 — solver-filled, attacker controls)
#   call args         → symbolic bytes (0x00...00 fill)
#
# vulnerability.json output:
# []
# NOTE: In production with Gigahorse vulnerability detection active:
# [{"vulnerability_type":"ArbitraryCall","confidence":"HIGH","visibility":"PUBLIC",
#   "key_statement":"0x76","key_selector":"0x1cff79cd",...}]
